Tracking macros helps you optimize body composition, performance, and overall health."]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Protein"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Protein builds and repairs muscle tissue. For active individuals, aim for ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"1.6-2.2g per kg of body weight"}],". Higher intake supports muscle growth during bulking and preserves lean mass during cutting."]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Carbohydrates"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Carbs are your body's primary energy source. They fuel workouts and replenish glycogen stores. Active individuals typically need ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"3-5g per kg"}]," depending on training intensity and goals."]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Fats"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Dietary fats support hormone production, nutrient absorption, and cell health. Aim for ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"0.8-1g per kg"}]," of body weight. Going below 15% of total calories can harm hormone levels."]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"How to Use This Calculator"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"Enter your body
